data.title: Fed's Daly Emphasizes Importance of Price Stability Without Economic Harm
data.description: Restoring price stability is crucial, but it should not come at the expense of the economy, according to Mary C. Daly, President of the San Francisco Fed. According to ChainCatcher, Daly highlighted the need to balance efforts to stabilize prices with maintaining economic health.

data.title: Alex Mashinsky Seeks to Overturn 12-Year Sentence for Fraud
data.description: Alex Mashinsky has filed a motion in a New York court to overturn his 12-year sentence for fraud and market manipulation. According to NS3.AI, Mashinsky submitted the motion without additional legal representation, following his announcement on May 5 that he would represent himself in the proceedings.

data.title: Crypto Policy Highlights: U.S. Regulation, Bermuda Blockchain, and European Banks
data.description: A recent analysis highlights five significant themes in the crypto policy landscape, focusing on developments in U.S. regulation, Bermuda's blockchain adoption, and European banking activities under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework. According to NS3.AI, the discussion around the CLARITY Act, concerns over the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) credibility, and Hester Peirce's anticipated departure from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) are pivotal in shaping U.S. regulatory approaches. Additionally, Bermuda's proactive policy efforts to integrate blockchain technology and Banca Sella's expansion of crypto services in Europe are seen as key advancements in the global crypto ecosystem.

data.title: Fed's Daly: AI Unlikely to Cause Mass Unemployment
data.description: Mary C. Daly, President of the San Francisco Federal Reserve, stated that large-scale unemployment or job losses are not expected due to artificial intelligence. According to Jin10, Daly's comments suggest a more optimistic outlook on the impact of AI on the labor market.

data.title: SEC Charges Texas Resident for Fraudulent AI Crypto Trading Scheme
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has filed a lawsuit against Nathan Fuller, a resident of Texas, accusing him of illegally raising approximately $12.3 million from around 150 investors through a fraudulent 'AI crypto trading robot' scheme. 

According to Odaily, the SEC stated that from October 2022 to mid-2024, Fuller sold crypto investment interests under the names Privvy Investments LLC and Gateway Digital Investments. He claimed to use an 'AI high-frequency arbitrage robot' for trading crypto assets, promising investors guaranteed returns of 40% to over 100% within 21 to 45 days.

The regulatory body alleged that Fuller falsely claimed that investment funds were protected by FDIC insurance, surety bonds, and professional liability insurance. In reality, the purported trading robot did not operate as advertised. The SEC accused Fuller of misappropriating at least $6.2 million of investor funds for personal expenses and using approximately $5.5 million from new investors for 'Ponzi-style payments.' He also allegedly misled investors by fabricating account statements and creating fictitious institutional communications.

The SEC has filed the lawsuit in the federal court for the Southern District of Texas, charging Fuller with violations related to securities issuance and anti-fraud laws. The commission is seeking a permanent injunction, disgorgement of ill-gotten gains, and civil penalties.

data.title: ExxonMobil VP Warns of Imminent Oil Price Surge
data.description: ExxonMobil's senior vice president, Neil Chapman, has warned that global oil inventory tightness could reach critical levels within weeks, potentially driving Brent crude prices to $150 or $160 per barrel. According to BeInCrypto, Chapman highlighted at a Bernstein investor conference that the market is nearing rarely seen stockpile levels, exacerbated by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. The International Energy Agency reported a drawdown of 246 million barrels in March and April, with cumulative supply losses possibly exceeding one billion barrels by month-end. Analysts suggest commercial inventories are weaker than data indicates, raising concerns about potential energy shortages.

data.title: U.S. Money Market Funds Reach Record $8.281 Trillion Amid Fed Uncertainty
data.description: Investors have increased the total size of U.S. money market funds to a record $8.281 trillion due to uncertainty surrounding the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. According to Odaily, Crane Data's latest figures show that approximately $66 billion flowed into the money market fund industry in the week ending May 28. Of this, around $41 billion was added on Thursday as investors adjusted their portfolios before the month's end. Since the beginning of the year, money market funds have attracted about $172 billion.

data.title: HypeStrat's Holdings Surpass $1 Billion as BitMine Faces $8 Billion Loss
data.description: HypeStrat, a cryptocurrency treasury company, has seen its holdings exceed $1 billion in unrealized gains as HYPE continues to reach new all-time highs. According to Odaily, this positions HypeStrat as the top-performing crypto treasury company. In contrast, Ethereum treasury company BitMine (BMNR) is experiencing significant losses, with unrealized losses exceeding $8 billion, marking the largest loss among all crypto treasury companies.

data.title: Wintermute Provides Liquidity for Prediction Markets as CFTC Approves Kalshi's Bitcoin Futures
data.description: Wintermute has confirmed its role in providing two-sided liquidity for event contracts on major prediction-market platforms. According to NS3.AI, the London-based firm handles over $3.5 trillion in trading volume annually. Sources indicate that the platforms include Polymarket and Kalshi. In a related development, the CFTC has issued an order permitting Kalshi to offer U.S. perpetual futures linked to Bitcoin's price.

data.title: SEC Chairman Outlines New Era of Digital Asset Regulation
data.description: SEC Chairman Paul S. Atkins announced that the SEC is progressing with a reform initiative termed the "New Era SEC," which emphasizes digital asset regulation and on-chain capital markets. According to NS3.AI, Atkins highlighted that the SEC has initiated Project Crypto with backing from the Trump administration. The SEC is also collaborating with the CFTC to align cryptocurrency regulations.

data.title: San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly to Speak Shortly
data.description: San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly, who is a voting member of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) in 2027, is scheduled to deliver a speech in ten minutes. According to Jin10, her remarks are anticipated to provide insights into the Federal Reserve's monetary policy direction.

data.title: SEC Chair Paul Atkins Outlines Regulatory Reforms at Reagan Economic Forum
data.description:
U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Paul S. Atkins discussed regulatory reforms at the 2026 Reagan National Economic Forum. According to Odaily, Atkins emphasized the SEC's efforts to modernize digital asset regulation, develop on-chain capital markets, and position the U.S. as a global crypto hub.

Atkins criticized the previous SEC's "regulatory hostility" towards the digital asset industry, which he claimed forced many crypto innovations overseas. Under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration, the SEC has initiated "Project Crypto" and partnered with the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission to coordinate on-chain market infrastructure and crypto regulation. The SEC has recently clarified which digital assets are considered securities and is advancing an innovative exemption mechanism for tokenized listed securities. Additionally, the SEC is examining how on-chain trading systems can fit within the existing regulatory framework.

Atkins also highlighted the SEC's plans to reduce "excessive disclosure" and regulatory burdens, aiming to "Make IPOs Great Again" by lowering compliance costs for listed companies and increasing IPO flexibility. He proposed repealing climate disclosure rules introduced by the previous administration. Atkins stated that the future of U.S. capital markets should be based on "free market and innovation-driven" principles, with regulatory bodies providing clear rules and legal certainty rather than stifling technological advancement.

data.title: Western Union Launches USDPT on Solana Blockchain
data.description: Western Union has introduced USDPT on the Solana blockchain in May 2026, facilitated by Anchorage Digital Bank. According to NS3.AI, the company has also unveiled a Digital Asset Network aimed at linking crypto wallets and exchanges to its payout infrastructure. A consumer-oriented product is expected to be rolled out in numerous markets. Meanwhile, the World Bank's data indicates that average remittance costs exceed 6%, and Western Union has reported a decline in income for the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin Short Position Yields $2 Million in Unrealized Profit
data.description: Evaded has recently closed an Ethereum short position while maintaining a 960 BTC short position with 30x leverage. According to NS3.AI, the Bitcoin short position currently holds approximately $2 million in unrealized profit, reflecting an 80% return rate.

data.title: ViaBTC Launches 10th Anniversary Campaign 'Proof of Decade'
data.description: ViaBTC Group, a leading global blockchain infrastructure provider, has announced the launch of its 10th-anniversary campaign, "Proof of Decade," marking a significant milestone since its founding in 2016. According to BeInCrypto, the campaign celebrates a decade of continuous development and reliable service to miners, traders, and crypto users worldwide. ViaBTC Pool, renowned for its top ranking in LTC/DOGE merged mining, ZEC mining, and BCH mining, supports over ten major PoW cryptocurrencies. The campaign includes community celebrations and a $100,000 prize pool initiative starting June 2, 2026.

data.title: CertiK CEO Warns of Risks from Unisolated AI Agents
data.description: CertiK CEO Ronghui Gu has issued a warning regarding the potential risks posed by unisolated AI agents. According to NS3.AI, Gu highlighted that these agents could lead to the exposure of sensitive files, credentials, and financial accounts. CertiK's investigations have uncovered numerous malicious skills, fake installers, and deceptive dependency packages on open agent utility hubs. This discovery underscores the importance of ensuring robust security measures in AI deployments to protect against such vulnerabilities.

data.title: Gold Surpasses US Dollar in Central Bank Reserves, Fidelity Reports
data.description: Fidelity Digital Assets has reported that gold has surpassed U.S. dollar assets in global central bank reserves following a significant decline of approximately 20% from around $5,600 per ounce. According to NS3.AI, Fidelity noted that Bitcoin is increasingly being used in cross-border trade settlements, indicating a move away from dollar-based systems. However, the anticipated outperformance of Bitcoin compared to gold has not yet been realized, the firm added.

data.title: Trump's Iran Deal Remarks Boost Markets, Oil Drops
data.description: President Trump announced on Truth Social that he is meeting in the 'Situation Room' to make a final determination on the Iran peace deal, and that the naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will be lifted. This news led to a more than 2% drop in WTI crude oil prices, now below $87 per barrel, according to CoinDesk. U.S. stocks, which were previously flat, are now showing modest gains, with the Nasdaq up 0.2%. Bitcoin (BTC) has also risen about $1,000 from the morning's lows, reaching $73,500.

data.title: Polymarket Partners with OneFootball to Launch Exclusive Football Prediction Market
data.description: Polymarket has announced an exclusive partnership with sports media platform OneFootball. According to Foresight News, this collaboration will allow OneFootball users to access Polymarket's football prediction market.

data.title: Brookings Fellow Warns of CFTC's Resource Needs Amid Crypto Legislation
data.description: Aaron Klein, a Brookings Institution fellow, has raised concerns about the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) ability to oversee digital markets as Congress considers expanding its authority over crypto assets. According to CoinDesk, Klein emphasized that the CFTC, originally designed for commodity futures, lacks the resources, independence, and coordination needed for its expanded role. He warned that without additional staff and funding, the agency might appear to regulate without effective oversight, risking past regulatory failures. Klein also criticized political influence in financial regulation.
